summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire and stellar Other One anchor a tight show
blurb: First set opens strong with Jack‑A‑Roe and builds into a 20‑minute Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain. The second set delivers a powerful Other One and tight Samson & Delilah, while Donna Jean’s backing vocals add extra sparkle. Overall performance is energetic and tightly locked in.
review: May 13, 1977 at Chicago’s Auditorium Theatre captures one of spring ’77’s high‑water marks. The band bursts out with "The Music Never Stopped" and quickly rolls into a bright "Ramble On Rose" before dropping the surprise debut of "Jack‑A‑Roe," which reviewers call a “magical opening.”  From there the set flows seamlessly into a legendary Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue that stretches twenty minutes, with Jerry “on fire” and Phil’s bass thundering “8 on the Richter scale.”  The second set keeps the momentum, launching straight into a gritty "Samson & Delilah" followed by a tight Bertha, then soaring through an inspired Estimated Prophet.  Drums and percussion lock in for a scorching Other One where Jerry delivers one of his best solos of the tour; Stella Blue provides a lyrical breather before the band rounds out the night with a rollicking "One More Saturday Night" and an encore U.S. Blues.  Donna Jean’s backing vocals, while debated, add extra sparkle to the mix, and Keith’s keyboards sit cleanly beneath the groove.  Despite a brief board spill around "Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad," the soundboard capture remains crisp, making this a must‑listen performance for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Fire segue, The Other One, and the debut Jack‑A‑Roe. Multiple sources highlight Jerry’s “on” tone, Phil’s powerful bass (“8 on the Richter scale”), and overall tight, energetic playing across the set.

best_recording.reason: Miller’s soundboard source offers the cleanest mix with minimal glitches, capturing the band’s tight performance and dynamic nuances.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue (≈20 min)
Jerry’s fire solo during The Other One
Debut of Jack‑A‑Roe in the first set
Tight rhythm and powerful bass throughout, especially on Samson & Delilah

band_performance.Jerry: On fire all night with great tone; standout solo during The Other One.
band_performance.Phil: Powerful bass work, especially on Scarlet/Fire and The Other One (described as hitting "8 on the Richter scale").
band_performance.Bob: Steps up energy on El Paso and provides solid rhythm throughout.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboard textures sit cleanly in the mix; Donna Jean's backing vocals spark debate but add sparkle.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums lock in tightly, delivering punchy fills without missing a beat.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady, driving groove that complements Mickey’s percussion.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
